meta.sourceOfTruth: This file feeds BOTH the Astro dashboard island (src/components/Dashboard.jsx) AND the standalone frontier-models-dashboard.html (regenerated by build-html.py). The weekly task edits this file, regenerates the HTML, and writes a new Markdown post in src/content/posts/. Do not hand-edit the dashboard component or the HTML for data changes.
meta.summary: First pass of the Frontier Models tracker: 43 models and tools across closed frontier, open frontier, small/on-device, agentic frameworks, and classifiers/encoders — with benchmarks, trade-offs, and a pipeline view of what's next.

items: Established tracking for 43 models & tools across 5 categories: frontier closed, frontier open, small/on-device, agentic frameworks, and classifiers/encoders.

Closed frontier: Claude Opus 4.8 leads the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index (61.4); GPT-5.5 #2 (~60.2) and SOTA on Terminal-Bench 2.0 (82.7%); Gemini 3.5 Flash GA with 3.5 Pro rolling out; Grok 4.3.
Open frontier is Chinese-led: DeepSeek V4 (MIT) 80.6% SWE-bench Verified; Kimi K2.6 leads open SWE-bench Pro (58.6); GLM-5.1; best US-open is NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra (AA Index 48).
Small models closing the gap: Phi-4-mini-reasoning (3.8B) rivals o1-mini; Gemma 4 runs native multimodal in <1GB on a phone.

Agents hit their '1.0 / GA' year (LangGraph, MS Agent Framework, Google ADK 2.0, OpenAI Agents SDK); OpenClaw became the most-starred repo in GitHub history; Manus's corporate model collapsed, leaving OpenManus.
Pipeline watch: GPT-5.6 (rumored), Gemini 3.5 Pro GA, Grok 5 (in training), DeepSeek R2 (uncertain), MiniMax M3 weights (pending), Llama 5 (~2027).

pipeline.name: OpenAI GPT-5.6
pipeline.note: Widely expected late June 2026 per prediction markets and a brief Codex routing-log sighting — but unconfirmed. GPT-5.5 remains the current confirmed flagship.

pipeline.name: Google Gemini 3.5 Pro
pipeline.note: Announced at I/O; GA expected June 2026 with a 2M-token context and a 'Deep Think' reasoning mode. The 3.5 Flash tier is already GA.

pipeline.name: xAI Grok 5
pipeline.note: Training on the Colossus 2 supercluster, reportedly up to 10 trillion parameters, with a Q2/Q3 2026 target. Parameter count and timeline are rumored, not official.

pipeline.name: DeepSeek R2
pipeline.note: A dedicated R2 reasoning model is rumored (a 32B variant was reported), but it doesn't appear in DeepSeek's official API list — treat as unconfirmed. V4's Think modes already cover much of the reasoning role.

pipeline.name: MiniMax M3 weights
pipeline.note: Announced June 1 with the top claimed open-weight intelligence score; full weight release and independent benchmark verification are still pending.

pipeline.name: Meta Llama 5 ('Avocado')
pipeline.note: Meta shipped its first closed model (Muse Spark) in Apr 2026; the open Llama roadmap now appears to lag the closed line. Behemoth (2T) was shelved.

pipeline.name: Anthropic Fable / Mythos line
pipeline.note: A new top 'Fable 5' tier launched Jun 9 2026 above Opus 4.8, with a gated 'Mythos 5' sibling via a limited research program. Headline figures are still firming up.

usecases.q: Best model for complex agentic coding
usecases.a: Claude (Opus 4.8 / Fable 5) leads SWE-bench and long-horizon coding; GPT-5.5 tops terminal and computer-use agents. For an open, self-hosted option, DeepSeek V4 or Kimi K2.6 are the strongest.
Gemini 3.5 Flash ($1.50/$9, ~4x speed) and Grok 4.3 ($1.25/$2.50) give the best price-to-intelligence. Among open models, a self-hosted Qwen3.6-27B is extremely cost-effective.
At the trillion-param tier: DeepSeek V4 (MIT), Kimi K2.6 (multimodal), or GLM-5.1 (MIT). For a single-GPU-friendly dense model, Qwen3.6-27B (Apache-2.0). Best US-open model: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra.

usecases.q: Best small model for on-device / mobile
usecases.a: Gemma 4 E2B/E4B (Apache-2.0, native audio+vision, sub-1GB) or Qwen3.5-4B (native multimodal). For raw speed, Liquid LFM2; for regulated edge, IBM Granite 4 Nano; on Apple devices, AFM 3.

usecases.q: Best tiny model for cheap reasoning
usecases.a: Phi-4-mini-reasoning (3.8B, MIT) rivals o1-mini on math and beats larger R1-distilled models — the best reasoning-per-parameter pick for high-volume or on-device math/science.
Managed: Gemini Embedding 001 (#1 MTEB). Open / self-hosted: Qwen3-Embedding-8B (#1 open, best code retrieval). On-device: EmbeddingGemma (runs in <200MB RAM).
Managed: Cohere Rerank 4 (Pro for accuracy, Fast for latency) or Zerank-2. Open / self-hosted: Qwen3-Reranker or bge-reranker-v2-m3.

usecases.q: Best classifier for short-text NLU / zero-shot
usecases.a: DeBERTa-v3 still has the highest raw accuracy for short-text classification and zero-shot NLI; ModernBERT when you need 8K context or speed; GLiNER2 for zero-shot 'extract any entity' NER.

gpt-oss-safeguard (open; interprets your policy at inference with visible reasoning) for flexible moderation; Llama Guard 4 for native text+image filtering; ShieldGemma for lightweight category checks.

usecases.q: Best framework to build production agents
usecases.a: LangGraph for durable graph orchestration; Microsoft Agent Framework or Google ADK for enterprise; OpenAI Agents SDK or smolagents for lightweight; Claude Agent SDK for Claude-native coding/ops agents.

OpenClaw is the most popular open personal agent (the most-starred repo ever) — but treat security seriously (it has had data-exfiltration findings; sandbox it). OpenManus is the open successor to the now-collapsed Manus for general task automation.
OpenHands is the top open-source coding-agent platform (~72% SWE-bench Verified with a strong backing model like Claude Sonnet 4.5).

watchlist.name: NeoBERT
watchlist.note: 250M modern encoder (Mila), ~89 GLUE — matches RoBERTa-large with ~100M fewer params. A strong compact ModernBERT alternative.
watchlist.why: Could become a go-to compact encoder; watch for adoption and a clear license.

watchlist.name: EuroBERT / mmBERT / MrBERT
watchlist.note: Multilingual modern encoders (210M–2.1B) covering 35–110+ languages, all built on the ModernBERT architecture; MrBERT (BSC, 2026) adds legal/bio/Spanish spinoffs.
watchlist.why: The fast-moving multilingual-encoder frontier; promote whichever wins production adoption.

watchlist.name: Ant Ling / Ring (1T)
watchlist.note: Ant Group's trillion-scale open MoEs; Ring-2.6-1T is a reasoning model with customizable inference intensity and extreme token efficiency (MIT, per secondary sources).
watchlist.why: Another credible Chinese trillion-param open entrant; verify license + benchmarks before promoting.

watchlist.name: Tencent Hunyuan 3 / StepFun / Baichuan
watchlist.note: More open / open-weight Chinese frontier models shipping fast (e.g. Hunyuan Hy3-preview 295B-A21B).
watchlist.why: The open frontier is crowded and Chinese-led; track which sustain momentum.

watchlist.name: Agno
watchlist.note: High-performance Python agent runtime (~40K★) claiming ~2µs agent instantiation; markets itself as far faster than LangGraph for high-concurrency swarms.
watchlist.why: Performance-first alternative to CrewAI/LangGraph; watch enterprise uptake.

watchlist.name: Pydantic AI
watchlist.note: Type-safe agent framework (v1.x stable; v2 in beta) emphasizing structured outputs and validation.
watchlist.why: Popular with the Pydantic crowd; promote to a full card when v2 lands.

watchlist.name: Zerank-2 (ZeroEntropy)
watchlist.note: Tops the Agentset reranker leaderboard (~1638 ELO), edging Cohere Rerank 4 on instruction-following.
watchlist.why: Newer managed reranker; watch availability and independent evals.

models.name: Claude (Opus 4.8)
models.tagline: Best-in-class agentic coding and long-horizon autonomy.

facts.v: Text, image, PDF in → text out

models.perfNote: Opus 4.8 leads the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index at 61.4 and posts ~88.6% SWE-bench Verified — the strongest agentic-coding family. A new top 'Fable 5' tier launched Jun 9 2026 with even higher coding scores (figures still settling).
keyFeatures: Always-on adaptive 'thinking' with an effort parameter
Long-horizon agents: memory, context editing, compaction, task budgets
strengths: Strong long-horizon autonomy and tool use
tradeoffs: Most expensive flagship pricing among peers
Sensitive-domain requests can be re-routed or refused
recent: Fable 5 / Mythos 5 launched Jun 9 2026 as a new top tier (details still firming up)
Fable 5 wired into GitHub Copilot at launch
pipeline: Mythos 5 (unsafetied sibling) gated via a limited-access research program
Continued push on computer-use and long-horizon agent reliability

models.name: GPT-5.5 (OpenAI)
models.tagline: SOTA agentic/terminal coding and computer use, cost-efficient.
models.perfNote: Tops Terminal-Bench 2.0 at 82.7% and sits #2 on the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index (~60.2). GPQA Diamond 93.6%, ARC-AGI-2 85.0%. Trails Claude on SWE-bench Pro but leads on agentic computer-use.
keyFeatures: High token efficiency at frontier intelligence
Stricter cyber safeguards + Trusted Access program
bestUseCases: Agentic coding and autonomous computer-use tasks
recent: GPT-5.5 Instant became the default ChatGPT model (May 2026)
pipeline: GPT-5.6 widely expected late June 2026 (rumored, unconfirmed)
Continued Codex / computer-use and cyber-defense expansion

models.name: Gemini 3.5 (Google)
models.tagline: Unmatched price/speed-to-intelligence and native multimodal.
models.metric: Frontier coding at Flash price + ~4x speed
models.perfNote: Gemini 3.5 Flash is GA (Terminal-Bench 2.1 76.2%, MCP Atlas 83.6%) — frontier-class coding at Flash pricing and ~4x speed. The flagship 3.5 Pro (2M context, Deep Think) is rolling out through June; its specs are targeted, not yet final.
keyFeatures: Deep ecosystem: Search AI Mode, Workspace, Android
strengths: Class-leading cost and latency for the intelligence
Largest context window among frontier labs
tradeoffs: Flagship 3.5 Pro not yet fully GA
Flash regresses on some pure-reasoning / long-context evals
Pro pricing and specs still unconfirmed
recent: Gemini 3.5 Flash GA + Antigravity + Gemini Spark at I/O (May 19 2026)
pipeline: Gemini 3.5 Pro GA expected June 2026 (2M context, Deep Think)
Gemini Spark 24/7 agent expanding to Ultra subscribers

models.name: Grok 4.3 (xAI)
models.tagline: Best price/performance and context size in its tier; native video.

facts.v: Text, image, native video in; voice

models.perfNote: AA Intelligence Index ~53 — strong for its low price tier (GPQA 90.1%). Trails the very top (Opus 4.8 / GPT-5.5 / Gemini) on absolute intelligence but wins on price-performance, context size, and native video.
strengths: Largest context among Western closed models
tradeoffs: Lower absolute intelligence than the top three
pipeline: Grok 5 in training on Colossus 2, reportedly up to 10T params — Q2/Q3 2026 target (rumored)
xAI says ~7 models in training

models.name: Amazon Nova 2
models.tagline: Price-performance plus deep AWS integration and customization.
models.perfNote: Genuinely proprietary, cheap, and 1M-context — but well below the frontier on intelligence indices (AA ~19–23 vs 53–61 for the leaders). Its strength is price-performance, AWS integration, and the Nova Forge custom-model service.
keyFeatures: Nova Forge — build custom models from Nova checkpoints
Nova Premier as a 'teacher' for distillation
pipeline: Expanding Nova Forge custom-model tooling

models.name: DeepSeek V4
models.tagline: The strongest open-weight model for coding and long context.
models.perfNote: Per its model card, 'the best open-source model available today' — SWE-bench Verified 80.6%, LiveCodeBench 93.5%, Codeforces 3206. New hybrid sparse attention cuts 1M-context inference to ~27% of the FLOPs and ~10% of the KV cache vs V3.
keyFeatures: Three reasoning modes: Non-think / Think High / Think Max
strengths: Best open coding and competitive programming
Huge context at low inference cost
tradeoffs: 1.6T params — impractical to self-host for most
Trails closed frontier on the hardest agentic / factuality tasks
bestUseCases: Open coding and math at frontier level
Cost-sensitive API via DeepSeek or providers
recent: Day-0 serving recipes on vLLM / SGLang
pipeline: DeepSeek R2 reasoning model rumored but absent from the official API list — treat as uncertain

models.name: Kimi K2.6 (Moonshot)
models.tagline: Open-weight leader on agentic coding and tool use; native multimodal.
models.perfNote: Open-weight leader on SWE-bench Pro (58.6 — beats GPT-5.4, Opus 4.6, Gemini 3.1 Pro) and HLE-with-tools (54.0). AA Intelligence Index 54. Native multimodal via a MoonViT vision encoder.
keyFeatures: Agent Swarm: up to 300 sub-agents / 4,000 steps
tradeoffs: Trails closed frontier on pure no-tool reasoning
bestUseCases: Open agentic coding and long-horizon tasks
pipeline: Continued Agent Swarm scaling and multimodal expansion

models.name: GLM-5.1 (Z.ai)
models.tagline: Top open coding/agentic model, trained fully on domestic chips.
models.perfNote: GLM-5 posts 77.8% SWE-bench Verified (beats Gemini 3 Pro, GPT-5.2); GLM-5.1 claims #1 open on SWE-bench Pro (58.4). Notably trained entirely on Huawei Ascend chips with zero NVIDIA dependency.
keyFeatures: Strong calibration ('knows when it doesn't know')
Leads open models on BrowseComp, Vending Bench 2
strengths: Frontier capability on fully domestic compute
tradeoffs: 744B — an API model for ~99% of users
pipeline: Continued GLM-5.x iterations; multimodal is a known gap to close

models.name: Qwen3.6 (Alibaba, open)
models.tagline: Exceptional intelligence-per-parameter across every size, Apache-2.0.
models.metric: 27B dense reportedly matches Claude 4.5 Opus on Terminal-Bench 2.0
models.perfNote: The Qwen3.6-27B dense model edges the 397B MoE on coding (SWE-bench Verified 77.2) and reportedly matches Claude 4.5 Opus on Terminal-Bench 2.0 — exceptional intelligence-per-parameter. Note: the truly-flagship Qwen3.7 Max (May 2026) is closed / API-only.
keyFeatures: Widest size + modality range of any open family
tradeoffs: Multimodal vs dense lines split across model numbers
recent: Qwen3.7 Max announced as a closed flagship (May 20 2026)
pipeline: Closed Qwen-Max line for the absolute frontier; continued open mid-size releases

models.name: Llama 4 (Meta)
models.tagline: Huge ecosystem, but now well off the open frontier.
models.perfNote: Llama 4 remains Meta's open offering with a massive ecosystem and 10M context (Scout), but at ~14 months old it has fallen well behind the open frontier. Meta shipped its first closed model (Muse Spark) in Apr 2026, signaling a strategic shift.
tradeoffs: ~14 months old — off the open frontier
Behemoth (2T) shelved; momentum shifted to closed Muse
recent: Meta Superintelligence Labs shipped closed 'Muse Spark' (Apr 8 2026)
pipeline: Llama 5 (codename 'Avocado') rumored ~2027; the open roadmap now lags the closed Muse line

models.name: Mistral Large 3
models.tagline: Largest Apache-2.0 open MoE; the EU/sovereign-AI flagship.
models.metric: #2 OSS non-reasoning on LMArena at launch
models.perfNote: The largest Apache-2.0 open MoE from a major lab — debuted #2 in OSS non-reasoning (#6 OSS overall) on LMArena. Best-in-class multilingual (non-EN/ZH) and the leading EU/sovereign option. Note: Mistral Medium 3.5 ships under a no-commercial license.
keyFeatures: Voxtral TTS audio + Leanstral proof agent
tradeoffs: Trails the Chinese open frontier on coding / agentic
Medium 3.5's no-commercial license is a trap
pipeline: A reasoning variant of Large 3 was 'coming soon' — verify if shipped

models.name: gpt-oss (OpenAI)
models.tagline: OpenAI's Apache-2.0 open weights — runs on a single GPU.
models.perfNote: OpenAI's first open LLM since GPT-2. The 120B ≈ o4-mini and 20B ≈ o3-mini on reasoning, with native MXFP4 quantization to run on a single GPU. At ~10 months old its AA Index (33) now trails Gemma 4, Nemotron 3, and the Chinese frontier.
strengths: Truly permissive + runs on consumer hardware
tradeoffs: ~10 months old and lagging newer open models
bestUseCases: Fine-tuning base with a permissive license
recent: Still the current open release; gpt-oss-safeguard classifiers added late 2025
pipeline: No announced successor yet

models.name: NVIDIA Nemotron 3
models.tagline: Best US open-weight model — fully open data, recipes, weights.
models.metric: AA Intelligence Index 48 — best US open model
models.perfNote: Nemotron 3 Ultra scores 48 on the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index — the highest of any US open-weight model (ahead of Gemma 4 31B at 39, gpt-oss-120B at 33; behind Kimi K2.6 at 54). NVIDIA releases weights, training data, recipes, and reward models.
keyFeatures: Full open release: data + recipes + reward models + NVFP4 variants
strengths: Best US open model on the AA Index
tradeoffs: Still trails the Chinese open frontier (~6 index pts)
recent: Nemotron 3 Ultra released Jun 4 2026 (announced at Computex)
pipeline: Continued size-ladder updates; Nano Omni multimodal line

models.name: MiniMax M3
models.tagline: Claims the top open-weight intelligence score — pending verification.
models.perfNote: MiniMax M3 claims the top open-weight Artificial Analysis Index (55, edging Kimi K2.6) with 1M context and native multimodality — but several outlets flag the benchmarks as unverified and the weights as pending full release. The most 'asterisked' entry here.
strengths: Potential #1 open-weight on the AA Index
tradeoffs: Weights not fully released at announcement
bestUseCases: Evaluation against other open frontier models
pipeline: Full open-weight release + independent benchmark verification pending

models.name: Microsoft Phi-4
models.tagline: Tiny models that match systems many times their size.
models.perfNote: Phi-4-mini-reasoning (3.8B) matches o1-mini and surpasses it on Math-500 and GPQA Diamond, beating larger R1-distilled models. The new Phi-4-reasoning-vision-15B 'matches or exceeds systems many times its size' on math/science with strong screen/UI grounding.
keyFeatures: Selective reasoning (think only when it helps)
strengths: Great fine-tuning base for math / science
tradeoffs: Smaller general-knowledge breadth than big models
pipeline: Phi reasoning/vision line expanding; no Phi-5 announced

models.name: Google Gemma 4 (small/edge)
models.tagline: Phone-to-server multimodal, now fully Apache-2.0.

facts.v: Text, image, video; audio on E-models

models.perfNote: Gemma 4's E-models run on phones — E2B is 0.84GB (text-only) with QAT, E4B ~2.2GB — with native audio+vision and built-in speculative decoding. The 31B (Apache-2.0) is a top-3 open model on the Arena text leaderboard and fits one 80GB H100.
keyFeatures: Apache-2.0 across the whole phone→server range
Native multimodal incl. audio at the edge
tradeoffs: Tops out at 31B — not a trillion-param competitor
pipeline: Diffusion-based and further edge-optimized variants

models.name: Qwen3.5 Small Series
models.tagline: A 9B that beats 3x-larger models; native multimodal from scratch.
models.perfNote: Per early coverage, the 9B outperforms the prior Qwen3-30B (3x larger) on MMLU-Pro/GPQA and matches gpt-oss-120B on several evals; the 4B/9B beat GPT-5-Nano on multimodal (MMMU-Pro). Native multimodality is baked in, not adapter-bolted. (Numbers are secondary-sourced.)
tradeoffs: Larger siblings split across model numbers
pipeline: Continued small-model refreshes alongside the larger Qwen line

models.name: HuggingFace SmolLM3
models.tagline: Fully open 3B — weights, data, and recipe all public.
models.metric: Punches above its weight at the 3B scale
models.perfNote: A strong 3B trained on 11.2T tokens with dual-mode reasoning and tool calling — and fully reproducible (weights, data, and training recipe all released). Still the latest SmolLM as of June 2026 (no 3.5/4 yet).
tradeoffs: Outpaced by Qwen3.5 / Gemma 4 on raw benchmarks
recent: SmolLM3-3B remains the current release; SmolVLM2 covers vision
pipeline: A SmolLM refresh would be the next milestone (none announced)

models.name: IBM Granite 4.0 Nano
models.tagline: Tiny hybrid-Mamba models built for regulated, on-device use.
models.perfNote: Hybrid Mamba-2/transformer design (only ~10% attention layers) cuts memory 70–80% vs transformer-only while keeping long-context reasoning. Built for regulated industries with ISO 42001 certification and cryptographic weight verification.
keyFeatures: Runs fully on laptops / phones / industrial devices
strengths: Big memory savings via hybrid SSM
tradeoffs: Lower raw benchmarks than Qwen/Gemma at size
pipeline: Continued Granite 4.x hybrid releases

models.name: Liquid AI LFM2
models.tagline: The on-device speed leader — ~2x faster decode than rivals.
models.perfNote: Built for real on-device agents: LFM2-1.2B is competitive with the 47%-larger Qwen3-1.7B while decoding ~2x faster on CPU; LFM2-VL runs vision up to 2x faster on GPU at matching benchmark scores. The reference for fastest on-device inference.
keyFeatures: Strong knowledge / math / instruction for size
pipeline: Expanding on-device MoE + multimodal lineup

models.name: Apple Foundation Models 3
models.tagline: On-device multimodal models with per-prompt sparse routing.
models.metric: Native multimodal on-device, no cloud round-trip
models.perfNote: AFM 3 Core (~3B) and Core Advanced run fully on-device. Core Advanced uses a novel sparsely-activated architecture via Instruction-Following Pruning — the full model lives in flash and routes per-prompt to work around flash→DRAM bandwidth limits.
keyFeatures: Swift Foundation Models Framework now takes image input
tradeoffs: Lower ceiling than cloud frontier models
pipeline: Continued on-device model + framework expansion

models.name: OpenClaw
models.tagline: The viral open personal AI agent — most-starred repo ever.
models.perfNote: The runaway 2026 adoption story — the most-starred software repo in GitHub history. Runs locally and uses messaging apps (WhatsApp, Telegram, Slack, +20 more) as its UI, executing shell/browser/API actions via a SKILL.md skills system. But it is a serious security lightning rod.
keyFeatures: Local execution: shell, browser, API in a loop
Live 'Canvas' + voice on macOS / iOS / Android
tradeoffs: Serious security risk (Cisco found a data-exfiltration skill)
China restricted state/bank use; 'too dangerous' for non-technical users
recent: Renamed Clawdbot → Moltbot → OpenClaw after a trademark complaint
Creator joined OpenAI (Feb 2026); a foundation now stewards it
pipeline: Foundation governance + security hardening are the focus

models.name: OpenManus (+ Manus)
models.tagline: Open clone of the Manus autonomous agent — now the durable path.
models.perfNote: An open initiative replicating Manus's autonomous task execution (planning + tool-use + execution, browser automation, real-time thinking UI). It became the durable open path after the proprietary Manus's corporate model collapsed (China blocked Meta's acquisition, Apr 2026).
strengths: Open replacement for a collapsed proprietary product
tradeoffs: Less polished than the original Manus product
Quality depends on the backing LLM
recent: Manus's Meta acquisition blocked by China's NDRC (Apr 27 2026); corporate model 'dead'
pipeline: Continued OpenManus + OpenManus-RL development

models.name: OpenHands
models.tagline: Top open-source SWE agent platform (formerly OpenDevin).
models.perfNote: The leading open-source coding agent — ~72% on SWE-bench Verified with Claude Sonnet 4.5 extended thinking via its CodeAct architecture. Evaluated/used by AMD, Apple, Google, Amazon, Netflix, and NVIDIA. Launched the broader OpenHands Index eval in Jan 2026.
keyFeatures: CodeAct: agent actions as executable code
tradeoffs: Best results need a strong (paid) backing model
Docs lag the fast-moving V1 line
pipeline: Expanding the OpenHands Index + enterprise platform

models.name: LangGraph / LangChain
models.tagline: The de-facto default for durable, graph-based agent orchestration.
models.perfNote: Both LangChain and LangGraph hit v1.0 GA on Oct 22 2025 — the first stable major release in the durable-agent space, with backward-compat guarantees. LangChain for fast agent building; LangGraph for stateful, resumable, checkpointed orchestration. Most 2026 production stacks use both.
tradeoffs: More concepts to learn than lightweight libs
Can feel heavy for simple agents
pipeline: 1.x feature releases toward an eventual 2.0

models.name: CrewAI
models.tagline: Role-based multi-agent 'crews' and event-driven 'flows'.
models.perfNote: Role-based multi-agent orchestration ('Crews' of role-playing agents + event-driven 'Flows'), independent of LangChain. ~47.8K stars, 27M+ PyPI downloads, ~2B executions in 12 months, and claimed use across roughly half the Fortune 500.
bestUseCases: Teams wanting a clean role model
recent: Series A led by Insight Partners; continued v1.x
pipeline: Enterprise platform + flows expansion

models.name: Microsoft Agent Framework
models.tagline: AutoGen + Semantic Kernel, unified into one GA runtime.
models.perfNote: Reached v1.0 GA on Apr 7 2026, merging AutoGen's agent abstractions with Semantic Kernel's enterprise features (state, telemetry, middleware) plus graph workflows. AutoGen and Semantic Kernel are now maintenance-only — this is their successor.
recent: v1.0 GA (Apr 7 2026); AutoGen + Semantic Kernel → maintenance mode
pipeline: Deeper Azure AI Foundry + protocol integration

models.name: OpenAI Agents SDK
models.tagline: Lightweight production multi-agent SDK (Swarm's successor).
models.perfNote: OpenAI's lightweight, production-ready multi-agent SDK and the successor to the deprecated Swarm. The Apr 15 2026 'Next Evolution' overhaul added native sandbox execution, a long-horizon harness, and a subagent primitive.
pipeline: Planned 'code mode'; subagent primitive maturing

models.name: Google ADK 2.0
models.tagline: Code-first, enterprise agent execution framework.
models.perfNote: Launched ADK 2.0 at Google I/O (May 19 2026) — a code-first, enterprise-scale execution framework (not just a toolkit) tied into Gemini Enterprise / Vertex. 2.0 adds a graph-based Workflow Runtime and on-device ADK for Android with Gemini Nano.
pipeline: Expanding Tools & Integrations ecosystem

models.name: smolagents (HuggingFace)
models.tagline: Barebones agents that 'think in code' — ~1,000 LOC core.
models.perfNote: A deliberately minimal (~1,000 LOC) agent library where agents write actions as Python code rather than JSON tool calls — HF reports ~30% fewer steps and higher accuracy on hard benchmarks. The common scaffold in GAIA-benchmark course projects.
recent: Grew from ~3K to ~26K stars; actively maintained
pipeline: Continued core + tool improvements

models.name: Claude Agent SDK
models.tagline: Build agents on the same harness that powers Claude Code.
models.perfNote: A production library for building agents on the same harness as Claude Code — file edits, bash, web tools, a tool-use loop with HITL checkpoints, subagents, persistent sessions, and first-class MCP. 2026 added Managed Agents (cron, vault secrets) and self-hosted sandboxes.
keyFeatures: File / bash / web tools + tool-use loop
tradeoffs: Separate Agent SDK billing from Jun 15 2026
recent: Managed Agents public beta; self-hosted sandboxes (2026)
pipeline: Expanding managed / hosted agent capabilities

models.name: Dify
models.tagline: Visual platform for agentic workflows, RAG, and LLMOps.
models.perfNote: A production-ready visual platform unifying RAG, agents, model management, and observability in one UI. ~138K+ GitHub stars, 800+ contributors, weekly releases, and 1M+ apps deployed — one of the most-starred AI projects.
tradeoffs: License has a few restrictions vs pure OSS
Less low-level control than code frameworks
pipeline: Deeper agent + observability features

models.name: n8n (AI agents)
models.tagline: Workflow automation with native AI agent nodes.
models.perfNote: A visual workflow-automation platform whose most-used node in 2026 is the AI Agent node (chat model + prompt + memory + tools) — enabling multi-step reasoning inside any workflow. 400+ integrations, $180M Series C, ~$2.5B valuation.
strengths: Connects AI to real systems easily
pipeline: Deeper agent + tool-calling features

models.name: ModernBERT
models.tagline: The modern encoder baseline — fast, long-context, code-aware.
models.metric: 8K context; SOTA base-encoder GLUE at release
models.perfNote: The de-facto modern encoder baseline (RoPE, GeGLU, alternating local/global attention, Flash Attention, 2T-token training incl. code). A Pareto improvement on speed-vs-accuracy over DeBERTa-v3 / RoBERTa, and the base for a large ecosystem of language/domain variants.
keyFeatures: First encoder trained on a large code corpus
recent: Spawned MrBERT, EuroBERT, BioClinical ModernBERT, ModernGBERT, TabiBERT, etc.
pipeline: Ongoing language / domain adaptations from the community

models.name: DeBERTa-v3
models.tagline: Still the accuracy king for short-text NLU and zero-shot NLI.
models.metric: ~91.4 GLUE average — still the top encoder
models.perfNote: Five years on, DeBERTa-v3 still posts the highest raw GLUE (~91.4) among encoder-only models — beating ModernBERT/NeoBERT on pure classification accuracy. It's the backbone of choice for zero-shot NLI and GLiNER. No official v4 exists.
recent: Remains a top performer; powers GLiNER2 (2025)
pipeline: No v4 announced — the ModernBERT family is the long-context alternative

models.name: Qwen3-Embedding
models.tagline: The open-weight embedding champion for RAG and code search.
models.perfNote: The open-weight embedding leader — the 8B hit #1 on MMTEB (70.58) and still tops MTEB-Code (80.68). 100+ languages, Matryoshka dims, 32K context, and first-class in vLLM/SGLang — making self-hosting the default cost path above ~100M tokens/month.
tradeoffs: API embedders are simpler for small volume
pipeline: Multimodal Qwen3-VL-Embedding (Jan 2026)

models.name: EmbeddingGemma
models.tagline: Best open on-device embedding model — runs in <200MB RAM.
models.metric: Best open multilingual model under 500M on MTEB
models.perfNote: The on-device embedding leader — the best open multilingual model under 500M on MTEB, 100+ languages, running in <200MB RAM quantized at sub-15ms on EdgeTPU. The default for private, offline RAG.
tradeoffs: Below big embedders on absolute MTEB
pipeline: Part of the broader Gemma on-device push

models.name: Gemini Embedding 001
models.tagline: #1 proprietary embeddings on MTEB and multilingual MMTEB.
models.perfNote: The top proprietary embedding model — #1 on current MTEB (68.32) and long #1 on multilingual MMTEB. The best default API embedder, with Matryoshka dims down to 256. (Note: open models' 70–72 figures are on an older, non-comparable leaderboard version.)
pipeline: Tied to the broader Gemini API roadmap

models.name: Qwen3-Reranker
models.tagline: The leading open cross-encoder reranker family.
models.perfNote: The leading open reranker family, built on Qwen3 — pairs with Qwen3-Embedding for a fully open, self-hostable retrieval stack. For managed APIs, Cohere Rerank 4 and Zerank-2 lead; for OSI-open self-hosting, this and bge-reranker-v2-m3 are the picks.
pipeline: Multimodal Qwen3-VL-Reranker (Jan 2026)

models.name: Cohere Rerank 4
models.tagline: Top managed reranker — accuracy (Pro) and latency (Fast).
models.perfNote: A top managed reranker (~1629 ELO on the Agentset leaderboard), offered as Rerank 4 Pro (accuracy) and Rerank 4 Fast (~1506 ELO, latency). The simplest high-quality option when you don't want to self-host; Zerank-2 narrowly leads on instruction-following.
strengths: Two points on the cost curve
tradeoffs: Zerank-2 edges it on some evals
pipeline: Continued domain-tuned rerankers

models.name: gpt-oss-safeguard
models.tagline: Open reasoning safety classifier — interprets your policy live.
models.perfNote: Open-weight reasoning-based safety classifiers (built on gpt-oss) that interpret a developer-provided policy at inference time with reviewable chain-of-thought — rather than a fixed taxonomy. A new paradigm for flexible, auditable content moderation.
pipeline: Part of OpenAI's open-safety tooling line

models.name: Llama Guard 4
models.tagline: Multimodal (text+image) safety classifier from Meta.
models.perfNote: A natively multimodal (text + image) safety classifier derived from Llama 4 Scout, aligned to the MLCommons hazards taxonomy — for filtering both prompts and responses, including images, in production guardrail stacks.
pipeline: Part of Meta's Purple Llama safety suite

models.name: GLiNER2
models.tagline: Zero-shot 'extract any entity' — beats fine-tuned LLMs.
models.metric: Beats DeBERTa-v3 and LLMs on zero-shot NER
models.perfNote: A DeBERTa-v3-based generalist NER + schema-driven extraction model that beats GLiClass, DeBERTa-v3, and fine-tuned LLMs/ChatGPT on zero-shot NER — the go-to flexible 'extract any entity' model, tiny and cheap to run.
keyFeatures: Zero-shot NER for arbitrary entity types
pipeline: Continued multi-task extraction features
